questions: Should Apple Pencil on iPad behave like a mouse (precise, no long-press needed) or like a finger? Current plan treats it as mouse based on pointerType === 'pen'.
Is there a preferred gesture library (e.g., use-gesture, hammer.js) or should this be built from scratch on Pointer Events? The codebase currently has no gesture library.
For the precision trim magnifier (Step 8), should it show actual video frames or a waveform/thumbnail strip? This affects complexity significantly.
Should the phone layout support landscape orientation with a wider timeline, or is portrait-only acceptable for the initial implementation?
Is there a minimum iOS/Android version target? Safari < 16.4 has different gesture event handling that affects pinch zoom implementation.

success_criteria.criterion: Single-finger horizontal drag on the timeline scrolls (does not select or move clips) on touch devices
Long-press on a clip selects it with visual feedback within 200ms on touch devices
Selected clip can be dragged to a new position after long-press confirmation on touch
Clip trim handles have effective touch target of at least 32px on touch devices
Two-finger pinch zooms the timeline horizontally on touch devices
All existing desktop mouse interactions continue to work identically (no regression)
All existing timeline tests pass without modification (unless test itself needs updating for new behavior)
iPad uses full layout (not condensed) with slide-over properties panel
Phone shows quick-edit overlay near selected clip for common actions
Precision trim mode provides at least 4:1 movement reduction for fine adjustments
Timeline clips have appropriate ARIA roles and labels for screen reader navigation
Gesture state machine has unit tests covering all state transitions
Touch interaction integration tests cover select, multi-select, drag, trim, and scroll disambiguation
No new runtime dependencies added (gesture handling built on Pointer Events)
Manual testing completed on iPhone Safari, iPad Safari, and Android Chrome
VoiceOver and TalkBack screen reader testing completed

assumptions: Apple Pencil and stylus input (pointerType === 'pen') will be treated as mouse-like: immediate selection on tap, no long-press timer, precise hit areas.
No gesture library will be added — built on raw Pointer Events + Touch Events (for pinch) to avoid new dependencies and match existing patterns.
The 200ms long-press threshold is a starting point; will be tunable via a constant. Industry standard ranges from 150ms (iOS) to 500ms (Android default).
Haptic feedback via navigator.vibrate() is progressive enhancement — works on Android, ignored on iOS Safari (which doesn't support it). iOS haptics would require a native wrapper.
Phone portrait mode is the primary phone layout; landscape will use the same layout but with more horizontal space (no special handling needed initially).
The existing condensed layout mode in VideoEditorShell is the correct base for phone; iPad uses the full desktop layout with touch-adapted interactions.
Precision trim magnifier (Step 8) will use thumbnail strips for video clips and waveform for audio clips, matching existing WaveformOverlay patterns.
Two-finger gestures (pinch, undo swipe) take priority over any single-finger gesture in progress — the arbitrator always cancels single-finger state when a second finger arrives.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
